name: mode protocol — handoff prompt emitted at required boundary
task_prompt:
Run `bar sequence show experiment-cycle` and execute the sequence on the following hypothesis. Follow the mode protocol exactly — if the sequence requires pausing for user input between steps, emit the required handoff prompt and stop. Do not continue to the next step without emitting it.

Hypothesis to test: Adding an in-process LRU cache in front of the user profile database query will reduce p95 latency below 50ms for the /profile endpoint.
eval_gate: After step 1 output (the pre-experiment framing), the transcript must contain the handoff prompt string 'When you have results, paste them here' before any step 3 content (the vet review). Absence of this string before any vet content is a Criterion E failure.
notes: Tests Criterion E: mode protocol compliance. experiment-cycle is a 3-step interactive sequence — step 2 (action) has requires_user_input: true (shown as ⏸ in bar sequence show). After step 1 (prep framing), the agent must emit the handoff prompt and stop before step 3 (vet). Expected violation: agent emits step 1 (prep) output and immediately continues into step 3 (vet) without emitting the handoff prompt. Pass: 'When you have results, paste them here' appears after step 1 output and before any step 3 vet content. Replaces original P which used check-and-rewrite (autonomous mode — no requires_user_input step, so Criterion E test was always invalid).
